title: ProcScript for Menus
text: ProcScript for Menus You control how a menu item is displayed by using the ProcScript instructions in the preDisplay trigger. Table 1. ProcScript Functions Function Description $check Setting $check to True places a checkmark before the menu item. Setting $check to False removes the checkmark before the menu item. If an icon has been defined for the menu item, the checkmark is placed over the icon. $check cannot be used in a menu bar. $disable Setting $disable to True disables the menu item. Setting $disable to False enables the menu item. A disabled menu item is visible but can’t be selected by the user. You can control how a disabled menu is presented in the menu properties. $hide Setting $hide to True hides the menu item. Setting $hide to False shows the menu item. $inlinemenu Menu items of type Dynamic menu are placeholders for menus that you define using ProcScript. You assign the definition of your menu to $inlinemenu . Table 2. ProcScript Statements Statement Description pulldown Use this statement to activate the menu when your Uniface application runs in character mode. pulldown/load Loads a new definition for the application menu bar. Related Links Menu Properties Menu properties enable you to control the appearance of menus using the font, colors, and images. They are applicable only for Windows applications and are defined by the menu logical in the [application] section of the application initialization file (usys.ini). Dynamic Menu Items Dynamic menus contain one or more menu items that are generated at runtime, and inserted in a predetermined location of a drop-down or popup menu. They can be used, for example, to display and open recently accessed objects, list and switch between currently open windows, or provide a pop-up menu that is related to the value of a field.
